title: Introduction to QAIDR
text: QAIDR (Quality Assessment Interval-based Dimensionality Reduction) provides tools evaluating interval DR methods using co-ranking matrices. supports: 4 interval distance metrics: Interval Euclidean, Hausdorff, Ichino-Yaguchi, L2-Wasserstein 3 quality/behavior index families: T&C, MRRE, LCMC (6 indices total) 6 DR methods: C-PCA, V-PCA, MR-PCA, SPCA, IMDS, Int-UMAP Statistical tests: Permutation-based significance testing

title: Introduction to QAIDR
text: Interval-valued data consists observations variable interval [,b][, b], represented internally center (+b)/2(+b)/2 radius (b−)/2(b-)/2. can also create interval data common formats:
code: library(QAIDR)  # From centers and radii matrices C <- matrix(rnorm(40), 10, 4) R <- matrix(runif(40, 0.1, 0.5), 10, 4) x <- interval_data(C, R, labels = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 5)) print(x) #> interval_data: 10 observations, 4 variables #> Labels: A, B # From a min-max data frame df <- data.frame(   A.min = c(1, 3, 5), A.max = c(2, 5, 8),   B.min = c(10, 20, 30), B.max = c(12, 25, 35),   class = c("x", "y", "x") ) x2 <- interval_data_from_mm(df, int_min_at = c(1, 3), y_at = 5) print(x2) #> interval_data: 3 observations, 2 variables #> Labels: x, y  # From a 3D array (n x p x 2) arr <- array(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 2, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9), dim = c(3, 2, 2)) x3 <- interval_data_from_array(arr)

title: Introduction to QAIDR
text: Standardize interval data variable zero-mean centers unit standard deviation:
code: xs <- standardize(x) # Column means of centers are now 0 round(colMeans(xs$centers), 10) #> [1] 0 0 0 0 # Column SDs of centers are now 1 round(apply(xs$centers, 2, sd), 10) #> [1] 1 1 1 1

title: Introduction to QAIDR
text: Four distance metrics available comparing interval-valued observations:
code: D1 <- idist(xs, metric = "Wasserstein") D2 <- idist(xs, metric = "Hausdorff") D3 <- idist(xs, metric = "Int-Euclidean") D4 <- idist(xs, metric = "Ichino-Yaguchi")  cat("Distance matrix dimensions:", dim(D1), "\n") #> Distance matrix dimensions: 10 10

title: Introduction to QAIDR
text: Given high-dimensional low-dimensional distance matrices, compute six quality/behavior indices: Quality indices (Q_TC, Q_RE, Q_LC): range [0, 1], higher better Behavior indices (B_TC, B_RE, B_LC): range [-1, 1], 0 means balanced
code: set.seed(42) Y <- matrix(rnorm(20), 10, 2) Dl <- as.matrix(dist(Y)) Dh <- idist(xs, metric = "Wasserstein")  indices <- coranking_indices(Dh, Dl, K = 3) print(indices) #>         Q_TC         B_TC         Q_RE         B_RE         Q_LC         B_LC  #>  0.393333333 -0.066666667  0.442941176 -0.003529412  0.200000000  0.666666667

title: Introduction to QAIDR
code: data(cars_mm) print(cars_mm) #> interval_data: 27 observations, 4 variables #> Labels: Berlina, Luxury, Sportive, Utilitarian summary(cars_mm) #> interval_data: 27 observations, 4 variables #>  #> Center summary: #>      Price            EngCap        TopSpeed      Acceleration    #>  Min.   : 21342   Min.   :1173   Min.   :157.0   Min.   : 4.500   #>  1st Qu.: 34420   1st Qu.:1681   1st Qu.:194.2   1st Qu.: 8.000   #>  Median : 60900   Median :2388   Median :213.5   Median : 9.000   #>  Mean   :102406   Mean   :2580   Mean   :216.0   Mean   : 9.667   #>  3rd Qu.:169238   3rd Qu.:3378   3rd Qu.:235.0   3rd Qu.:11.500   #>  Max.   :315992   Max.   :4530   Max.   :296.5   Max.   :14.500   #>  #> Radii summary: #>      Price            EngCap          TopSpeed      Acceleration   #>  Min.   :  2850   Min.   :  78.5   Min.   : 0.50   Min.   :0.000   #>  1st Qu.:  6064   1st Qu.: 259.8   1st Qu.: 6.50   1st Qu.:0.500   #>  Median : 11890   Median : 447.0   Median :12.00   Median :1.000   #>  Mean   : 31869   Mean   : 591.7   Mean   :11.07   Mean   :1.333   #>  3rd Qu.: 42689   3rd Qu.: 822.2   3rd Qu.:14.00   3rd Qu.:2.000   #>  Max.   :160081   Max.   :1720.5   Max.   :24.50   Max.   :4.000   #>  #> Label distribution: #>  #>     Berlina      Luxury    Sportive Utilitarian  #>           8           4           8           7  data(facedata_mm) print(facedata_mm) #> interval_data: 27 observations, 6 variables #> Labels: FRA, HUS, INC, ISA, JPL, KHA, LOT, PHI, ROM

title: Introduction to QAIDR
text: typical QAIDR workflow : Load create interval_data Standardize standardize() Run DR methods run_idr() Assess quality assess_quality() Visualize plot_projections() plot_k_profiles() See “Real Data Analysis” “Simulation Study” vignettes complete examples.
code: # Complete example (requires symbolicDA, RSDA) data(cars_mm) x <- standardize(cars_mm)  proj <- run_idr(x) result <- assess_quality(x, proj, K = 5, perm_test = TRUE, n_perm = 1000) print(result)  plot_projections(proj, labels = cars_mm$labels)  profiles <- k_profiles(x, proj, K_max = 20) plot_k_profiles(profiles, metric = "Wasserstein")

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
text: Cars dataset contains 27 car models described 4 interval-valued variables (Price, Engine Capacity, Top Speed, Acceleration) 4 class labels (Berlina, Luxury, Sportive, Utilitarian).
code: library(QAIDR) set.seed(2025)  data(cars_mm) print(cars_mm) summary(cars_mm)

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
code: x <- standardize(cars_mm)

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
code: proj <- run_idr(x, labels = cars_mm$labels) print(proj)

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
text: DR method produces 2D projection intervals displayed rectangles:
code: plot_projections(proj,                  labels = cars_mm$labels,                  obs_labels = rownames(cars_mm$centers))

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
text: Evaluate 6 methods across 4 metrics neighbourhood size K = 5, 1000 permutations significance testing: asterisk (*) indicates statistical significance 0.05 level.
code: result <- assess_quality(x, proj, K = 5,                          perm_test = TRUE, n_perm = 1000) print(result)

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
text: Quality behavior indices across neighbourhood sizes K:
code: profiles <- k_profiles(x, proj)  # Plot for each metric for (met in c("Int-Euclidean", "Hausdorff", "Ichino-Yaguchi", "Wasserstein")) {   plot_k_profiles(profiles, metric = met) }

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
text: Face dataset contains 27 individuals described 6 interval-valued anthropometric measurements (AD, BC, AH, DH, EH, GH).
code: data(facedata_mm) print(facedata_mm)

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
code: x_face <- standardize(facedata_mm) proj_face <- run_idr(x_face, labels = facedata_mm$labels)

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
code: plot_projections(proj_face,                  labels = facedata_mm$labels,                  obs_labels = rownames(facedata_mm$centers))

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
code: result_face <- assess_quality(x_face, proj_face, K = 5,                               perm_test = TRUE, n_perm = 1000) print(result_face)

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
code: profiles_face <- k_profiles(x_face, proj_face) plot_k_profiles(profiles_face, metric = "Wasserstein")

title: Real Data Analysis with QAIDR
text: assessment table reports six indices method-metric combination: Quality indices closer 1 indicate better structure preservation. Behavior indices near 0 indicate balanced intrusions extrusions; large positive negative values indicate directional bias embedding.

title: Simulation Study with QAIDR
code: library(QAIDR) set.seed(2025)  methods_list <- c("C-PCA", "V-PCA", "MR-PCA", "SPCA", "IMDS", "Int-UMAP") metrics_list <- c("Int-Euclidean", "Hausdorff", "Ichino-Yaguchi", "Wasserstein")  # Set N_PERM = 1000 for final paper results N_PERM <- 10

title: Simulation Study with QAIDR
text: Tests indices respond different radii magnitudes. Three groups 100 observations small, medium, large interval widths.
code: N <- 300; P <- 5 centers <- matrix(rnorm(N * P), N, P) radii <- matrix(0, N, P)  # Group 1: Small radii (0.1 -- 0.2) radii[1:100, ] <- matrix(runif(100 * P, 0.1, 0.2), 100, P) # Group 2: Medium radii (1.0 -- 1.2) radii[101:200, ] <- matrix(runif(100 * P, 1.0, 1.2), 100, P) # Group 3: Large radii (5.0 -- 6.0) radii[201:300, ] <- matrix(runif(100 * P, 5.0, 6.0), 100, P)  colnames(centers) <- paste0("V", seq_len(P)) colnames(radii) <- paste0("V", seq_len(P))  x_s1 <- interval_data(centers, radii) x_s1_std <- standardize(x_s1)

title: Simulation Study with QAIDR
text: concisely assess_quality():
code: proj_s1 <- run_idr(x_s1_std)  res_s1 <- data.frame() for (met in methods_list) {   cat("Processing:", met, "\n")   out <- proj_s1[[met]]    for (metric in metrics_list) {     Dh <- idist(x_s1_std, metric = metric)     Dl <- if (out$type == "Point") {       as.matrix(dist(out$C))     } else {       idist(out$C, out$R, metric)     }      pt <- perm_test(Dh, Dl, K = 10, n_perm = N_PERM)     res_s1 <- rbind(res_s1, data.frame(       Method = met, Metric = metric,       Q_TC = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["Q_TC"]),       B_TC = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["B_TC"]),       Q_RE = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["Q_RE"]),       B_RE = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["B_RE"]),       Q_LC = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["Q_LC"]),       B_LC = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["B_LC"])     ))   } } print(res_s1) result_s1 <- assess_quality(x_s1_std, proj_s1, K = 10,                             perm_test = TRUE, n_perm = N_PERM) print(result_s1)

title: Simulation Study with QAIDR
text: Tests well DR methods preserve structure nonlinear manifold data interval-valued.
code: dat_sr <- gen_interval_swissroll(n = 800, seed = 42) print(dat_sr)

title: Simulation Study with QAIDR
code: dat_sr_std <- standardize(dat_sr) proj_sr <- run_idr(dat_sr_std)  res_s2 <- data.frame() for (met in methods_list) {   cat("Processing:", met, "\n")   out <- proj_sr[[met]]    for (metric in metrics_list) {     Dh <- idist(dat_sr_std, metric = metric)     Dl <- if (out$type == "Point") {       as.matrix(dist(out$C))     } else {       idist(out$C, out$R, metric)     }      pt <- perm_test(Dh, Dl, K = 10, n_perm = N_PERM)     res_s2 <- rbind(res_s2, data.frame(       Method = met, Metric = metric,       Q_TC = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["Q_TC"]),       B_TC = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["B_TC"]),       Q_RE = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["Q_RE"]),       B_RE = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["B_RE"]),       Q_LC = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["Q_LC"]),       B_LC = sprintf("%.3f", pt$vals["B_LC"])     ))   } } print(res_s2)

title: Simulation Study with QAIDR
text: Tests robustness indices small perturbations interval radii. Mean Absolute Error (MAE) sign-flip rates computed 100 iterations.
code: N <- 200; P <- 5; K <- 10; N_ITER <- 100 center_sd <- 0.05; r_min <- 1.2; r_max <- 2.5; eps <- 0.25  # Generate base data with dense overlap centers_base <- matrix(rnorm(N * P, mean = 0, sd = center_sd), N, P) radii_base <- matrix(runif(N * P, r_min, r_max), N, P) colnames(centers_base) <- paste0("V", seq_len(P)) colnames(radii_base) <- paste0("V", seq_len(P))  x_base <- interval_data(centers_base, radii_base) xs_base <- standardize(x_base) proj_base <- run_idr(xs_base)

title: Simulation Study with QAIDR
code: stability <- data.frame()  for (met in names(proj_base)) {   for (mn in metrics_list) {     # Baseline indices     Dh0 <- idist(xs_base, metric = mn)     out <- proj_base[[met]]     Dl <- if (out$type == "Point") {       as.matrix(dist(out$C))     } else {       idist(out$C, out$R, mn)     }     base_vals <- coranking_indices(Dh0, Dl, K = K)      # Perturb radii N_ITER times     vals <- matrix(NA, nrow = N_ITER, ncol = 6)     for (i in seq_len(N_ITER)) {       mult_noise <- matrix(runif(N * P, -eps, eps), N, P)       r_pert <- radii_base * (1 + mult_noise)       r_pert[r_pert < 1e-8] <- 1e-8       Dh <- idist(centers_base, r_pert, mn)       vals[i, ] <- coranking_indices(Dh, Dl, K = K)     }      # MAE     mae <- colMeans(abs(vals - matrix(base_vals, nrow = N_ITER,                                       ncol = 6, byrow = TRUE)))      # Sign-flip rate for behavior indices (columns 2, 4, 6)     sign_flip <- c(       mean(sign(vals[, 2]) != sign(base_vals[2])),       mean(sign(vals[, 4]) != sign(base_vals[4])),       mean(sign(vals[, 6]) != sign(base_vals[6]))     )      stability <- rbind(stability, data.frame(       Method = met, Metric = mn,       MAE_Q_TC = mae[1], MAE_B_TC = mae[2],       MAE_Q_RE = mae[3], MAE_B_RE = mae[4],       MAE_Q_LC = mae[5], MAE_B_LC = mae[6],       SignFlip_B_TC = sign_flip[1],       SignFlip_B_RE = sign_flip[2],       SignFlip_B_LC = sign_flip[3]     ))   } }  print(stability)

title: Simulation Study with QAIDR
text: reproducible paper results, set N_PERM = 1000 N_ITER = 100. eval = FALSE default avoids long computation vignette building. Copy code R session required packages installed run interactively. Scenario III designed dense overlap (center_sd = 0.05, r_min = 1.2) stress-test stability indices.

what: Quality Assessment for Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction
title: Quality Assessment for Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction
text: Quality Assessment Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction QAIDR provides tools evaluating well interval-based dimensionality reduction (DR) methods preserve structure high-dimensional interval data, using co-ranking matrix framework.

title: Quality Assessment for Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction
text: 4 interval distance metrics: Interval Euclidean, Hausdorff, Ichino-Yaguchi, L2-Wasserstein 6 co-ranking indices: Quality (Q) Behavior (B) variants Trustworthiness & Continuity, MRRE, LCMC 6 DR method wrappers: C-PCA, V-PCA, MR-PCA, SPCA, IMDS, Int-UMAP Permutation tests statistical significance Visualization: 2D projection plots K-neighbourhood profile plots

title: Quality Assessment for Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("hanmingwu1103/QAIDR")

title: Quality Assessment for Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction
code: library(QAIDR)  # Load and standardize the built-in Cars dataset data(cars_mm) x <- standardize(cars_mm)  # Compute interval distances D <- idist(x, metric = "Wasserstein")  # Run all 6 DR methods (requires symbolicDA, RSDA) proj <- run_idr(x)  # Assess quality across all method-metric combinations result <- assess_quality(x, proj, K = 5, perm_test = TRUE, n_perm = 1000) print(result)  # Visualize plot_projections(proj, labels = cars_mm$labels)  profiles <- k_profiles(x, proj) plot_k_profiles(profiles, metric = "Wasserstein")

title: Quality Assessment for Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction
text: Package website vignette("introduction") – Basic workflow vignette("real-data-analysis") – Reproduces real data analysis vignette("simulation-study") – Reproduces simulation study

title: Quality Assessment for Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction
text: MIT

what: QAIDR: Quality Assessment for Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction — QAIDR-package
title: QAIDR: Quality Assessment for Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction — QAIDR-package
text: Provides quality behavior indices evaluating interval-based dimensionality reduction (DR) methods using co-ranking matrices. Supports multiple interval distance metrics (Euclidean, Hausdorff, Ichino-Yaguchi, L2-Wasserstein) assessment criteria (Trustworthiness & Continuity, MRRE, LCMC). Includes wrappers symbolic PCA methods, interval MDS, interval UMAP, well permutation tests statistical significance.

title: QAIDR: Quality Assessment for Interval-Based Dimensionality Reduction — QAIDR-package
text: Maintainer: Han-Ming Wu hmwu@stat.ntpu.edu.tw (ORCID)

what: Assess quality of interval DR projections — assess_quality
title: Assess quality of interval DR projections — assess_quality
text: Computes quality behavior indices combination DR method interval distance metric. Optionally performs permutation tests statistical significance.

title: Assess quality of interval DR projections — assess_quality
code: assess_quality(   x,   projections,   K = 5,   metrics = c("Int-Euclidean", "Hausdorff", "Ichino-Yaguchi", "Wasserstein"),   perm_test = FALSE,   n_perm = 1000 )

title: Assess quality of interval DR projections — assess_quality
text: x interval_data object (standardized). projections idr_projections object run_idr(), named list structure. K Integer neighborhood size (default 5). metrics Character vector distance metrics evaluate. perm_test Logical; perform permutation tests (default FALSE). n_perm Integer number permutations (default 1000).

title: Assess quality of interval DR projections — assess_quality
text: object class qaidr_assessment containing: results Data frame columns IDR, Metric, Q_TC, B_TC, Q_RE, B_RE, Q_LC, B_LC. pvalues Data frame p-values (perm_test = TRUE). K neighborhood size used.

title: Assess quality of interval DR projections — assess_quality
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(cars_mm) x <- standardize(cars_mm) proj <- run_idr(x) result <- assess_quality(x, proj, K = 5, perm_test = TRUE) print(result) } # }

title: Cars interval data — cars_mm
text: Interval-valued measurements 27 car models across 4 variables: Price, Engine Capacity (EngCap), Top Speed, Acceleration. observation interval representing range values within car class.

title: Cars interval data — cars_mm
code: cars_mm

title: Cars interval data — cars_mm
text: interval_data object 27 observations 4 variables: centers 27 x 4 matrix interval midpoints. radii 27 x 4 matrix interval half-widths. labels Factor 4 levels: Berlina, Luxury, Sportive, Utilitarian.

title: Cars interval data — cars_mm
text: Billard, L. Diday, E. (2006). Symbolic Data Analysis: Conceptual Statistics Data Mining. Wiley.

what: Compute co-ranking quality and behavior indices — coranking_indices
title: Compute co-ranking quality and behavior indices — coranking_indices
text: Given high-dimensional low-dimensional distance matrices, computes quality (Q) behavior (B) indices based co-ranking matrix framework. Three index families computed: Trustworthiness & Continuity (T&C), Mean Relative Rank Errors (MRRE), Local Continuity Meta-Criterion (LCMC).

title: Compute co-ranking quality and behavior indices — coranking_indices
code: coranking_indices(Dh, Dl, K)

title: Compute co-ranking quality and behavior indices — coranking_indices
text: Dh Numeric matrix high-dimensional pairwise distances (n x n). Dl Numeric matrix low-dimensional pairwise distances (n x n). K Integer neighborhood size.

title: Compute co-ranking quality and behavior indices — coranking_indices
text: named numeric vector elements Q_TC, B_TC, Q_RE, B_RE, Q_LC, B_LC.

title: Compute co-ranking quality and behavior indices — coranking_indices
code: set.seed(42) X <- matrix(rnorm(50), 10, 5) Y <- matrix(rnorm(20), 10, 2) Dh <- as.matrix(dist(X)) Dl <- as.matrix(dist(Y)) coranking_indices(Dh, Dl, K = 3) #>        Q_TC        B_TC        Q_RE        B_RE        Q_LC        B_LC  #> 0.653333333 0.066666667 0.670000000 0.008235294 0.500000000 0.266666667

what: Generate interval Swiss roll data — gen_interval_swissroll
title: Generate interval Swiss roll data — gen_interval_swissroll
text: Generates synthetic interval-valued dataset embedded Swiss roll manifold. Useful benchmarking interval dimensionality reduction methods.

title: Generate interval Swiss roll data — gen_interval_swissroll
code: gen_interval_swissroll(   n = 800,   seed = 1,   t_min = 1.5 * pi,   t_max = 4.5 * pi,   y_max = 70,   noise_sd = 0.02,   r_min = 0.2,   r_max = 0.5 )

title: Generate interval Swiss roll data — gen_interval_swissroll
text: n Integer number observations (default 800). seed Integer random seed (default 1). t_min Minimum angle parameter (default 1.5 * pi). t_max Maximum angle parameter (default 4.5 * pi). y_max Maximum value linear dimension (default 70). noise_sd Standard deviation Gaussian noise centers (default 0.02). r_min Minimum interval radius (default 0.2). r_max Maximum interval radius (default 0.5).

title: Generate interval Swiss roll data — gen_interval_swissroll
text: interval_data object 3 variables (x, y, z).

title: Generate interval Swiss roll data — gen_interval_swissroll
code: dat <- gen_interval_swissroll(n = 100, seed = 42) print(dat) #> interval_data: 100 observations, 3 variables

title: Compute interval distance matrix — idist
text: Dispatcher computes distance matrix using named interval metric.

title: Compute interval distance matrix — idist
code: idist(centers, radii = NULL, metric = "Wasserstein")

title: Compute interval distance matrix — idist
text: centers Numeric matrix interval midpoints (n x p), interval_data object. radii Numeric matrix interval half-widths (n x p). Ignored centers interval_data object. metric Character string: one "Int-Euclidean", "Hausdorff", "Ichino-Yaguchi", "Wasserstein".

title: Compute interval distance matrix — idist
text: symmetric n x n distance matrix.

title: Compute interval distance matrix — idist
code: C <- matrix(rnorm(12), 4, 3) R <- matrix(runif(12, 0.1, 0.5), 4, 3) D <- idist(C, R, "Wasserstein")

title: Interval Euclidean distance — idist_euclidean
text: Computes interval Euclidean distance interval-valued observations, defined weighted combination maximum minimum distances hyperrectangles.

title: Interval Euclidean distance — idist_euclidean
code: idist_euclidean(centers, radii, lambda = 0.5)

title: Interval Euclidean distance — idist_euclidean
text: centers Numeric matrix interval midpoints (n x p). radii Numeric matrix interval half-widths (n x p). lambda Weight parameter \([0, 1]\) (default 0.5).

title: Interval Euclidean distance — idist_euclidean
text: symmetric n x n distance matrix.

title: Interval Euclidean distance — idist_euclidean
code: C <- matrix(rnorm(12), 4, 3) R <- matrix(runif(12, 0.1, 0.5), 4, 3) D <- idist_euclidean(C, R)

code: idist_hausdorff(centers, radii)

title: Hausdorff distance for intervals — idist_hausdorff
text: centers Numeric matrix interval midpoints (n x p). radii Numeric matrix interval half-widths (n x p).

title: Hausdorff distance for intervals — idist_hausdorff
text: symmetric n x n distance matrix.

title: Hausdorff distance for intervals — idist_hausdorff
code: C <- matrix(rnorm(12), 4, 3) R <- matrix(runif(12, 0.1, 0.5), 4, 3) D <- idist_hausdorff(C, R)

title: Ichino-Yaguchi dissimilarity for intervals — idist_ichino_yaguchi
text: Computes Ichino-Yaguchi dissimilarity interval-valued observations based intersection union intervals.

code: idist_ichino_yaguchi(centers, radii, gamma = 0.5)

title: Ichino-Yaguchi dissimilarity for intervals — idist_ichino_yaguchi
text: centers Numeric matrix interval midpoints (n x p). radii Numeric matrix interval half-widths (n x p). gamma Weighting parameter (default 0.5).

title: Ichino-Yaguchi dissimilarity for intervals — idist_ichino_yaguchi
text: symmetric n x n distance matrix.

title: Ichino-Yaguchi dissimilarity for intervals — idist_ichino_yaguchi
code: C <- matrix(rnorm(12), 4, 3) R <- matrix(runif(12, 0.1, 0.5), 4, 3) D <- idist_ichino_yaguchi(C, R)

title: L2-Wasserstein (Mallows) distance for intervals — idist_wasserstein
text: Computes L2-Wasserstein distance interval-valued observations, assuming uniform distributions within interval.

code: idist_wasserstein(centers, radii)

title: L2-Wasserstein (Mallows) distance for intervals — idist_wasserstein
text: centers Numeric matrix interval midpoints (n x p). radii Numeric matrix interval half-widths (n x p).

title: L2-Wasserstein (Mallows) distance for intervals — idist_wasserstein
text: symmetric n x n distance matrix.

title: L2-Wasserstein (Mallows) distance for intervals — idist_wasserstein
code: C <- matrix(rnorm(12), 4, 3) R <- matrix(runif(12, 0.1, 0.5), 4, 3) D <- idist_wasserstein(C, R)

title: Create an interval_data object — interval_data
text: Constructs interval_data object center radii matrices.

title: Create an interval_data object — interval_data
code: interval_data(centers, radii, labels = NULL)

title: Create an interval_data object — interval_data
text: centers numeric matrix interval midpoints (n x p). radii numeric matrix interval half-widths (n x p). labels optional factor character vector class labels (length n).

title: Create an interval_data object — interval_data
text: object class interval_data.

title: Create an interval_data object — interval_data
code: C <- matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6), nrow = 3) R <- matrix(c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3), nrow = 3) x <- interval_data(C, R)

what: Create interval_data from a 3D min-max array — interval_data_from_array
title: Create interval_data from a 3D min-max array — interval_data_from_array
text: Converts 3D array (n x p x 2) [,,1] lower bound [,,2] upper bound interval_data object.

title: Create interval_data from a 3D min-max array — interval_data_from_array
code: interval_data_from_array(arr, labels = NULL)

title: Create interval_data from a 3D min-max array — interval_data_from_array
text: arr 3D numeric array dimensions (n, p, 2). labels optional factor character vector class labels.

title: Create interval_data from a 3D min-max array — interval_data_from_array
text: interval_data object.

title: Create interval_data from a 3D min-max array — interval_data_from_array
code: arr <- array(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 2, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9),              dim = c(3, 2, 2)) x <- interval_data_from_array(arr)

what: Create interval_data from a min-max data frame — interval_data_from_mm
title: Create interval_data from a min-max data frame — interval_data_from_mm
text: Reads data frame alternating min/max columns extracts centers radii. corresponds former extract_CR_from_MM.

title: Create interval_data from a min-max data frame — interval_data_from_mm
code: interval_data_from_mm(mm_df, int_min_at, y_at = NULL)

title: Create interval_data from a min-max data frame — interval_data_from_mm
text: mm_df data frame min/max column pairs. int_min_at Integer vector column indices interval minima. y_at Integer NULL. Column index class labels.

title: Create interval_data from a min-max data frame — interval_data_from_mm
text: interval_data object.

title: Create interval_data from a min-max data frame — interval_data_from_mm
code: df <- data.frame(   A.min = c(1, 3), A.max = c(2, 5),   B.min = c(10, 20), B.max = c(12, 25),   class = c("a", "b") ) x <- interval_data_from_mm(df, int_min_at = c(1, 3), y_at = 5)

what: Compute quality/behavior index profiles over K — k_profiles
title: Compute quality/behavior index profiles over K — k_profiles
text: Computes co-ranking indices neighborhood sizes 1 K_max, combination DR method distance metric.

title: Compute quality/behavior index profiles over K — k_profiles
code: k_profiles(   x,   projections,   K_max = NULL,   metrics = c("Int-Euclidean", "Hausdorff", "Ichino-Yaguchi", "Wasserstein") )

title: Compute quality/behavior index profiles over K — k_profiles
text: x interval_data object (standardized). projections idr_projections object. K_max Maximum neighborhood size (default nrow(x$centers) - 2). metrics Character vector distance metrics.

title: Compute quality/behavior index profiles over K — k_profiles
text: data frame columns Method, Metric, K, Q_TC, B_TC, Q_RE, B_RE, Q_LC, B_LC.

title: Compute quality/behavior index profiles over K — k_profiles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(cars_mm) x <- standardize(cars_mm) proj <- run_idr(x) profiles <- k_profiles(x, proj, K_max = 10) } # }

what: Permutation test for a single DR projection — perm_test
title: Permutation test for a single DR projection — perm_test
text: Performs permutation test assess statistical significance quality behavior indices.

title: Permutation test for a single DR projection — perm_test
code: perm_test(D_high, D_low, K, n_perm = 1000)

title: Permutation test for a single DR projection — perm_test
text: D_high High-dimensional distance matrix (n x n). D_low Low-dimensional distance matrix (n x n). K Integer neighborhood size. n_perm Number permutations (default 1000).

title: Permutation test for a single DR projection — perm_test
text: list elements: vals Named vector observed index values. pQ P-values quality indices (one-tailed). pB P-values behavior indices (two-tailed).

title: Permutation test for a single DR projection — perm_test
code: set.seed(42) Dh <- as.matrix(dist(matrix(rnorm(50), 10, 5))) Dl <- as.matrix(dist(matrix(rnorm(20), 10, 2))) pt <- perm_test(Dh, Dl, K = 3, n_perm = 99)

what: Plot quality/behavior index profiles over K — plot_k_profiles
title: Plot quality/behavior index profiles over K — plot_k_profiles
text: Creates 2 x 3 grid line plots (Quality Behavior T&C, MRRE, LCMC) given distance metric.

title: Plot quality/behavior index profiles over K — plot_k_profiles
code: plot_k_profiles(profile_data, metric = NULL)

title: Plot quality/behavior index profiles over K — plot_k_profiles
text: profile_data data frame returned k_profiles(). metric Character string specifying distance metric plot. NULL, returns list plots metrics.

title: Plot quality/behavior index profiles over K — plot_k_profiles
text: ggplot (list plots metric NULL).

title: Plot quality/behavior index profiles over K — plot_k_profiles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(cars_mm) x <- standardize(cars_mm) proj <- run_idr(x) profiles <- k_profiles(x, proj, K_max = 10) plot_k_profiles(profiles, metric = "Wasserstein") } # }

title: Plot 2D interval projections — plot_projections
text: Creates faceted plot 2D interval projections multiple DR methods, displaying intervals rectangles.

title: Plot 2D interval projections — plot_projections
code: plot_projections(projections, labels = NULL, obs_labels = NULL)

title: Plot 2D interval projections — plot_projections
text: projections idr_projections object named list. labels factor character vector class labels. obs_labels Optional character vector observation labels text.

title: Plot 2D interval projections — plot_projections
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot 2D interval projections — plot_projections
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(cars_mm) x <- standardize(cars_mm) proj <- run_idr(x) plot_projections(proj, labels = cars_mm$labels) } # }

what: Run interval dimensionality reduction methods — run_idr
title: Run interval dimensionality reduction methods — run_idr
text: Applies multiple interval DR methods (C-PCA, V-PCA, MR-PCA, SPCA, IMDS, Int-UMAP) interval_data object returns projections.

title: Run interval dimensionality reduction methods — run_idr
code: run_idr(   x,   methods = c("C-PCA", "V-PCA", "MR-PCA", "SPCA", "IMDS", "Int-UMAP"),   labels = NULL,   no_dim = 2,   umap_config = umap_config_default(),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run interval dimensionality reduction methods — run_idr
text: x interval_data object (standardized). methods Character vector methods run. Default includes six: "C-PCA", "V-PCA", "MR-PCA", "SPCA", "IMDS", "Int-UMAP". labels Optional factor class labels. NULL, uses x$labels. no_dim Integer number target dimensions (default 2). umap_config List UMAP hyperparameters. Default uses umap_config_default(). verbose Logical; print progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Run interval dimensionality reduction methods — run_idr
text: object class idr_projections: named list element contains C (centers matrix), R (radii matrix), type ("Point" "Interval").

title: Run interval dimensionality reduction methods — run_idr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(cars_mm) x <- standardize(cars_mm) proj <- run_idr(x) } # }

title: Standardize interval data — standardize
text: Centers midpoints scales midpoints radii midpoint standard deviation per variable. standard symbolic standardization procedure.

title: Standardize interval data — standardize
code: standardize(x)

title: Standardize interval data — standardize
text: x interval_data object.

title: Standardize interval data — standardize
text: new interval_data object standardized values.

title: Standardize interval data — standardize
code: C <- matrix(rnorm(30), 10, 3) R <- matrix(runif(30, 0.1, 1), 10, 3) x <- interval_data(C, R) xs <- standardize(x)

code: umap_config_default()

code: cfg <- umap_config_default()

what: Strong UMAP configuration for interval UMAP — umap_config_strong

code: umap_config_strong()
